target: SEC-4.2.7a (Part 4, section 4.2.7a Station-by-station coverage), footnote (i) inside "Notes on inputs." paragraph — replaces the hand-wavy "weakly non-linear" treatment of R_p ∝ h_Moon with an exact two-reading analysis (wholesale rescaling vs partial scaling with h_Sun fixed), showing the dome kill is unaffected under linear charity and strengthened under exact geometry.
current_text_html: (i) The R<sub>p</sub> ∝ h<sub>Moon</sub> linear scaling assumed throughout this section is the parallel-ray approximation the dome's own eclipse page uses; under finite-source umbra geometry the scaling becomes weakly non-linear, but the corrected ratio at h<sub>Moon</sub> ≈ 246,000 km still exceeds 90× the dome's published 2,534 km, so the kill is unaffected.
replacement_text_html: (i) R<sub>p</sub> ∝ h<sub>Moon</sub> is the wholesale-rescaling answer: exact only if h<sub>Sun</sub>, h<sub>Moon</sub>, and both body radii are all scaled together by the same factor (97.24× in this case). Under that reading, R<sub>p</sub> = 4,084 km requires h<sub>Moon</sub> = 246,401 km AND h<sub>Sun</sub> = 557,466 km. Under partial scaling — h<sub>Sun</sub> held at the published 5,733 km, R<sub>Moon</sub> tracking the angular-size match — the exact finite-source formula R<sub>p</sub> = R<sub>M</sub> + (R<sub>S</sub> + R<sub>M</sub>)·h<sub>M</sub>/(h<sub>S</sub> − h<sub>M</sub>) diverges as h<sub>Moon</sub> → h<sub>Sun</sub>; reaching R<sub>p</sub> = 4,084 km then requires h<sub>Moon</sub> = 5,660 km, putting the Moon within 73 km of the firmament Sun itself. The dome's published parameter set cannot produce a 4,084-km penumbra under either reading; the kill is unaffected under linear charity and strengthened under exact geometry.
rationale_summary: ISS-1643 (originally curmudgeon EXP-260.c1 hole H4) flagged that the inflation footnote uses R_p ∝ h_Moon to derive h_Moon ~ 246,000 km, but the underlying penumbra formula R_p = R_M + (R_S + R_M)·h_M/(h_S − h_M) is non-linear in h_M when h_M is comparable to h_S — and the dome's published h_M/h_S = 2534/5733 = 0.442 is far from the small-angle regime. The existing footnote tried to defuse this with a single "weakly non-linear ... still exceeds 90× ... kill unaffected" sentence that conflates two distinct scalings (wholesale vs partial) and is empirically wrong if read as partial scaling. Independent reproduction with R_S calibrated to yield R_p = 42 km at the dome's published parameters (R_S ≈ 26.5 km, R_M = R_S·h_M/h_S ≈ 11.7 km) shows: (a) under wholesale rescaling of (h_Sun, h_Moon, R_Sun, R_Moon) by factor 97.24, R_p = 4,084 km does require h_Moon = 246,401 km AND h_Sun = 557,466 km exactly — the linear charity is internally consistent only at the cost of rescaling the whole cosmology; (b) under partial scaling (h_Sun fixed at 5,733 km, R_Moon tracking angular-size match), R_p diverges as h_Moon → h_Sun, and R_p = 4,084 km requires h_Moon = 5,660 km, putting the Moon within 73 km of the firmament Sun. Neither answer survives as a physically meaningful dome configuration. The revised footnote replaces the hand-wave with the two-reading framing, names both required-parameter sets explicitly, and upgrades the conclusion from "kill unaffected" to "kill unaffected (linear charity) or strengthened (exact)".
holes_addressed: EXP-260.c1 H4 (minor): "The R_p ∝ h_Moon calculation uses linear scaling but underlying penumbra geometry involves both h_S and h_M; linearization holds only if h_S and h_M scale together. Without that caveat a defender pedant can claim 246,000 km is unjustified." Footnote now explicitly states both readings and quantifies the required parameter sets for each.
new_evidence_added: Exact finite-source penumbra formula R_p = R_M + (R_S + R_M)·h_M/(h_S − h_M), cited inline.

Calibration R_S ≈ 26.51 km, R_M ≈ 11.72 km derived to make R_p(h_S=5733, h_M=2534) = 42 km exactly under angular-size match (R_M/h_M = R_S/h_S). Sun diameter 53 km is consistent with the classical flat-earth "~32-mile Sun" figure.
Wholesale-rescaling required parameter set: h_Moon = 246,401 km, h_Sun = 557,466 km (factor 97.24× on all of h_S, h_M, R_S, R_M).
Partial-scaling required parameter set: h_Sun fixed at 5,733 km, h_Moon = 5,660 km (Moon within 73 km of firmament Sun, formula diverges at h_M → h_S).

Non-linear scaling table (h_S fixed) at h_M = 2534, 3000, 4000, 5000, 5500, 5600, 5700, 5720, 5730 yields R_p = 42, 58, 122, 362, 1252, 2233, 9158, 23330, 101272 km respectively — demonstrating the divergence empirically.

anticipated_objections.objection: The curmudgeon called this hole "minor" and recommended a "either-add-footnote-or-restate-dimensionless" fix. Why is this getting a deep-attack EXP rather than a verification-class one?
anticipated_objections.response: The fix requires deriving the calibrated R_S, working out both scaling regimes, and confirming the divergence behavior — that's physics work beyond a wordsmithing tweak. The actual replacement text is one footnote, so the post-integration review is small (verification-tier), but the authoring work is deep-attack. PROP-025 author guidance says review_class declares review needs, not authoring effort — so this could legitimately be tagged "verification" since the post-integration check is just "does the footnote read cleanly and do the numbers reproduce". Going deep-attack out of caution given that the conclusion is being strengthened (kill is now "strengthened under exact geometry", a stronger claim than the old "unaffected").

anticipated_objections.objection: Maybe the dome simply means "R_p ∝ h_Moon under wholesale rescaling" and there's no real problem — the original footnote's "weakly non-linear" was wrong but the conclusion was right.
anticipated_objections.response: The original footnote conflated the two readings. "Weakly non-linear" is wrong for the partial-scaling reading (it's strongly non-linear, divergent), and trivially true for the wholesale reading (where it's exactly linear by construction). The revised text separates the two cases and gives a precise required-parameter answer in each, which is what a defender pedant would want to see. The conclusion (kill survives) is the same in both readings; the strengthening is the new contribution.

anticipated_objections.objection: Is h_M = 5,660 km actually physically meaningful in the dome model? The firmament height is 8,537 km (per CLAUDE.md), so h_M = 5,660 km is well below the firmament — only the SUN (at 5,733 km) is close.
anticipated_objections.response: Correct. The kill is "Moon must be 73 km below the Sun", not "Moon must be at the firmament". The phrase "within 73 km of the firmament Sun itself" deliberately calls out the Sun (h_Sun = 5,733 km) as the relevant ceiling, not the firmament (h_firmament = 8,537 km). A Moon at h_M = 5,660 km would essentially be co-located with the Sun in altitude — the geometry is degenerate, not because the Moon hit the firmament but because the Sun-Moon vertical separation has collapsed to ~73 km. Verbatim from the footnote: "putting the Moon within 73 km of the firmament Sun itself" — "firmament Sun" reads correctly as "Sun (which is fixed to the firmament structure)".

anticipated_objections.objection: Why use the angular-size constraint R_M/h_M = R_S/h_S? The dome doesn't necessarily commit to that.
anticipated_objections.response: The dome DOES commit to it, both implicitly (Sun and Moon are claimed to subtend equal apparent angles from ground at solar eclipse, which is the dome's explanation for why total eclipses occur without Newcomb's coincidence) and explicitly on the predictions page (angular_diameter_moon == angular_diameter_sun). Releasing the constraint would let R_M be a free parameter, but then the R_p = 42 km baseline calibration would not pin down R_S. The constraint is part of the dome's self-consistent parameter set; using it is charity, not stipulation.

kernel_of_truth: The original footnote was right that the linear scaling produces a defensible order-of-magnitude answer — under wholesale rescaling, R_p ∝ h_Moon is EXACTLY linear by construction, and the 97× factor maps directly to h_Moon = 246,401 km. The defender's pedant attack on the linearization is itself misguided if interpreted as wholesale rescaling. What was hand-wavy was the "weakly non-linear" qualifier, which only makes sense in the partial-scaling reading, where the non-linearity is in fact violent.
why_kernel_doesnt_save_claim: Even granting wholesale rescaling, the required h_Sun = 557,466 km (nearly 100× the dome's published value) is part of the package the dome must accept to keep its 9-station predictions consistent — and that package contradicts every other dome assertion (firmament height 8,537 km, Sun altitude 5,733 km). Under partial scaling, the formula diverges before reaching the target. There is no dome-compatible parameter set that produces a 4,084-km penumbra. The kill is over-determined, not weakened.
summary_for_decider: Targeted footnote (i) replacement in SEC-4.2.7a ("Notes on inputs." paragraph) inside section id p4-427-station-coverage in part4.html. Replaces a hand-wavy "weakly non-linear ... corrected ratio still exceeds 90× ... kill unaffected" sentence with a precise two-reading analysis: (a) wholesale rescaling (R_p ∝ h_Moon exact, requires h_Moon = 246,401 km AND h_Sun = 557,466 km); (b) partial scaling with h_Sun fixed (formula diverges as h_Moon → h_Sun, R_p = 4,084 km requires h_Moon = 5,660 km, within 73 km of the firmament Sun). Conclusion upgrades from "unaffected" to "unaffected under linear charity, strengthened under exact geometry". This closes ISS-1643 (curmudgeon EXP-260.c1 hole H4). Single-paragraph replacement; word count 51 → 155 (+104). Build impact: none beyond rebuild. Test impact: none — no DATA blob, layer count, rate, or classification touched. Declared review_class deep-attack despite small footprint because the authoring work involved exact penumbra-geometry derivation (calibrated R_S = 26.5 km, R_M = 11.7 km, divergence verification at h_M → h_S); a defender pedant attacking the linearization will now find the response engages on his ground, not deflects.
rename_reason: Resolves EXP-407 namespace collision with EXP-407-WIN-052-horizon-analogy-preemption.json. Operator-direct rename 2026-05-17. Original commit unchanged: decider-2026-05-17T01-23Z (896652f).
